The three-year agreement represents more than $40 billion of lending in Euro-denominated loans, freeing up capital to support Klarna’s continued growth.

Bros."},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/klarna-plans-buy-now-pay-073012242.html","title":"Klarna Plans New Buy-Now-Pay-Later SRT as It Targets US Growth","content":"4 days ago — In April, Klarna and investors led by Varde Partners LP struck an SRT deal tied to $1.7 billion worth of euro-denominated loans. The ...Read more
(Bloomberg) -- Klarna is seeking to offload credit risk tied to a portfolio of 'buy now, pay later loans', in a deal aimed at freeing up capital as it looks to accelerate its international expansion plans.
The Stockholm-based lender is working on an SRT tied to around 5 billion kroner ($516 million) of loans originated by its Swedish unit, according to people familiar with the matter.
Banks use SRTs as a way to insure loans against default, typically obtaining protection for between 5% and 15% of the portfolio value.
Blackstone Inc and Sona Asset Management are among those firms increasing their exposure to such instruments as they evolve from transactions mostly tied to corporate loans transactions to deals involving more specialized loans.
Earlier this year, Banco Santander SA discussed with investors an SRT tied to around €500 million of loans made by its digital banking arm Openbank to clients in Germany."},{"url":"https://investors.klarna.com/News--Events/news/news-details/2026/Klarna-Agrees-to-1-7-Billion-Transaction-Supporting-up-to-40B-of-Lending/default.aspx","title":"Klarna Agrees to $1.7 Billion Transaction Supporting up to ...","content":"Apr 1, 2026 — Klarna Agrees to $1.7 Billion Transaction Supporting up to $40B+ of Lending April 1, 2026 ・ covering $1.7 billion-worth of Euro denominated ..."}]}

The deal follows Klarna’s recent announcement of a $2 billion facility supporting expansion of its U.S. financing operations to $17 billion. The company has doubled its loan sale agreement with Elliott Investment Management to $2 billion, which is anticipated to support up to $17 billion in U.S. financing loans over the program’s life.
